common_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
Add feed_start_date and feed_end_date to feed_info.txt and include calendar end dates in your export.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_input_files_in_subfolder' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_column' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_email' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
